food_subgroup: Filled bread / sandwich with egg and vegetables

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No specific FoodOn term exists for Banh Mi. FOODON_00002473 (sandwich food product) is the closest parent category covering filled bread products.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKP (Sandwiches) is the closest parent. No specific Banh Mi code exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226559009 (Sandwich) is the closest parent concept. No specific Banh Mi concept exists in SNOMED CT.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard Banh Mi sandwich with sunny side up egg and salad, approximately 220 g, as typically served at a Vietnamese café in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Egg sandwich / filled baguette with egg and salad — composite estimate derived from USDA FoodData Central entries: Hard-Boiled Egg (FDC ID 173424), French Bread / Baguette (FDC ID 172687), and Salad vegetables (lettuce, cucumber, tomato) with condiments
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact HPB Singapore or FAO/INFOODS entry for Banh Mi with sunny side up egg and salad was found. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data: baguette (~55% by weight), sunny side up egg (~25% by weight), mixed salad vegetables and condiments (~20% by weight). Values are blended per 100 g of the assembled sandwich. This is a medium-confidence composite estimate.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — white bread / baguette analogous estimate; Atkinson FS, Brand-Miller JC, Foster-Powell K, Buyken AE, Goletzke J. Am J Clin Nutr. 2021
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Banh Mi with egg and salad. The GI is estimated from the dominant carbohydrate component: Vietnamese baguette, which is a white wheat flour bread comparable to French baguette (GI ~70, University of Sydney GI Database). The egg and salad components contribute negligible glycemic load. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (70 × 60.5) / 100 = 42.4, where carbohydrate per 220 g serving = 27.5 × 2.2 = 60.5 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~310 mg per 100 g; ~682 mg per serving) from bread, condiments, and pickled vegetables
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should be aware that one serving provides approximately 682 mg sodium (~30% of the 2300 mg daily limit); request reduced condiments and limit pickled vegetable portions to manage sodium intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (~70) driven by refined white wheat baguette; high glycemic load per serving (~42)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume this dish with caution due to its high GI white baguette base and high glycemic load per serving (~42); pairing with additional non-starchy vegetables and limiting portion size can help moderate postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat (~1.8 g per 100 g) and cholesterol (~115 mg per 100 g) from egg yolk; moderate sodium from condiments
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For individuals with cardiovascular disease, the egg yolk contributes dietary cholesterol (~253 mg per serving) and saturated fat; consumption should be limited to occasional intake and paired with heart-healthy unsaturated fat sources while monitoring overall sodium from condiments.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Banh Mi with Sunny Side Up Egg and Salad (Vegetarian) is a Vietnamese-style filled baguette sandwich served at Vietnamese cafés in Singapore. The dish features a crispy, airy Vietnamese baguette — lighter and crispier than a standard French baguette due to a blend of wheat and rice flour — filled with a freshly pan-fried sunny side up egg, fresh lettuce, sliced cucumber, tomato, pickled daikon and carrot (đồ chua), fresh coriander, spring onion, and condiments such as mayonnaise, butter, and chilli sauce. The result is a harmonious combination of textures: the crunch of the baguette crust, the soft richness of the egg yolk, the crispness of fresh vegetables, and the tang of pickled garnishes. Nutritionally, this dish is a moderate-calorie meal (~436 kcal per 220 g serving) providing a meaningful source of protein (~17 g per serving) from the egg, carbohydrates (~60 g per serving) primarily from the baguette, and micronutrients including iron, vitamin A, and B vitamins. The high glycemic index (~70) driven by the refined white baguette makes portion control important for individuals managing blood glucose. Sodium from condiments and pickled vegetables (~682 mg per serving) warrants attention for those with hypertension. The dish is vegetarian but not vegan due to the egg and possible dairy in condiments. It is not gluten-free. In Singapore, this dish is commonly served as a breakfast or light lunch item at Vietnamese-themed cafés and food delivery platforms, reflecting the city-state's multicultural food landscape.

confidence_reason: No exact HPB Singapore or FAO/INFOODS entry for this specific Banh Mi variant was found. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data for baguette, sunny side up egg, and salad vegetables. GI is estimated from the analogous white baguette GI value from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium due to the composite estimation approach.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates derived from USDA FoodData Central component data (baguette ~55%, egg ~25%, salad and condiments ~20% by weight). No exact match was found in HPB Singapore Foodo or FAO/INFOODS SEA for this specific dish. 2. trans_fat_g is 0.0; this is confirmed zero for this egg-and-vegetable filled baguette with no industrially hydrogenated fats in the standard recipe — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central component data. 3. GI of 70 is an analogous estimate based on the dominant carbohydrate source (white wheat baguette) from the University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2021 (Am J Clin Nutr). 4. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, and all B vitamins are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data and are approximate. 5. The dish is served at Viet'Spice - Hương vị Việt - Circular Road on field survey data at SGD 15.90. 6. Cuisine is classified as Vietnamese-Singaporean reflecting the Vietnamese culinary origin served in a Singapore context. 7. The food_group has been corrected from the input 'Vegetables' to 'Mixed' as this is a filled bread sandwich with egg, bread, and vegetables.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Corrected food_name from menu-style 'BM4. Sunny Side Egg with salad (Vegetarian)' to canonical 'Banh Mi with Sunny Side Up Egg and Salad (Vegetarian)'.
Iteration 1: Corrected food_group from 'Vegetables' to 'Mixed' to reflect the composite nature of the dish.
Iteration 1: Added all mandatory multilingual local names: Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, Hindi, Vietnamese.
Iteration 1: Built comprehensive aliases and aliases_by_language with full consistency check.
Iteration 1: Estimated nutrient values from USDA FoodData Central component data for baguette, egg, and salad.
Iteration 1: Set GI to 70 (high) based on analogous white baguette estimate from University of Sydney GI Database.
Iteration 1: Documented trans_fat_g = 0.0 as confirmed zero in notes.
Iteration 1: Added all three required health context conditions with specific clinical guidance.
Iteration 1: Wrote LLM-ready natural language description exceeding 200 characters.

Iteration 2: Verified aliases_by_language consistency — all strings in aliases_by_language confirmed present verbatim in flat aliases list. Verified gi_category 'high' correct for GI=70. Verified all -1 sentinel values are for fields estimated from components and documented in notes.
